mnemonics: CRS Conflict (Conflicting Operations Rule for Serializability): A schedule is conflict serializable if you can reorder its non-conflicting operations (like R-W on different items) to form a serial schedule without changing the transaction order for conflicting ones (R-W, W-R, W-W on the same item).
keyPoints: A serial schedule is one where the operations of one transaction are executed completely before starting the operations of the next transaction.
Conflict Serializability is a schedule that is equivalent to some serial schedule when only conflicting operations (read-write, write-read, write-write on the same data item) are considered.

View Serializability is a broader concept where a schedule is view serializable if it is view equivalent to a serial schedule, meaning it has the same initial reads, same final writes, and same read-from relationships.
commonMistakes: Assuming that a serializable schedule must be serial. Serializable schedules are concurrent but have the same effect as a serial schedule.
Confusing Conflict Serializability with View Serializability. All conflict-serializable schedules are view serializable, but not all view serializable schedules are conflict serializable.
